symptoms: Small tan to dark brown pustules on lower leaf surfaces, yellow lesions on upper leaf surfaces, premature defoliation and yield loss
treatment: Apply triazole or strobilurin fungicides at early detection; scout fields regularly during humid conditions; plant resistant varieties where available; avoid planting near infected fields

symptoms: Interveinal chlorosis and necrosis on upper leaves, root rot with blue fungal growth, premature defoliation and stunted growth
treatment: Plant resistant varieties; improve field drainage to reduce soil moisture; rotate crops with corn or small grains; manage soybean cyst nematode populations

symptoms: Yellowing and stunting of plants in circular patches, reduced nodulation on roots, tiny white to yellow lemon-shaped cysts on roots
treatment: Plant resistant cultivars with appropriate race genes; rotate with non-host crops like corn; sample soil to monitor nematode levels; use nematicide seed treatments

symptoms: Water-soaked dark lesions on leaves and stems, white fuzzy sporulation on leaf undersides in humid conditions, tuber rot with reddish-brown dry decay
treatment: Apply mancozeb or chlorothalonil protectively; destroy volunteer potatoes and infected debris; ensure adequate hilling to protect tubers; plant certified disease-free seed

symptoms: Dark brown concentric target-like spots on older leaves, yellowing and necrosis surrounding lesions, stem cankers near the soil line
treatment: Apply azoxystrobin or chlorothalonil fungicides; rotate with non-solanaceous crops; maintain adequate fertility especially potassium; vine kill before harvest to reduce tuber infection

symptoms: Rough corky lesions on tuber surfaces, shallow to deep pits on harvested potatoes, scab-like raised areas that do not decay in storage
treatment: Maintain soil pH below 5.5 where feasible; use resistant varieties; ensure adequate soil moisture during tuber initiation; rotate with non-host crops like cereals

symptoms: Water-soaked angular lesions on leaves bounded by veins, blackened veins and stem cankers, boll infection with dark sunken spots and yellow ooze
treatment: Plant acid-delinted certified seed; apply copper-based bactericides early in season; practice crop rotation with non-malvaceous crops; destroy crop residues after harvest

symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of lower leaves in a V-shaped pattern, brown vascular discoloration in stems, stunted growth and reduced boll development
treatment: Plant tolerant or resistant varieties; rotate with cereal or grass crops; optimize irrigation to avoid water stress; avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization

symptoms: Yellowing and drooping of leaves on one side of the plant, vascular browning in roots and stems, stunted growth and premature plant death
treatment: Plant resistant cultivars with Fusarium wilt resistance genes; rotate with corn or sorghum; avoid root-knot nematode damage through nematode management; improve soil drainage

symptoms: Brown circular lesions with yellow halos on older leaves, small dark specks in center of lesions, premature defoliation starting from lower canopy
treatment: Apply chlorothalonil or tebuconazole beginning 30-40 days after planting; rotate with corn or cotton; maintain good plant spacing for air circulation; harvest promptly to avoid late-season defoliation

symptoms: Dark brown to black circular lesions on upper leaf surfaces, rough or fuzzy sporulation on lower leaf surfaces, severe defoliation and reduced peg strength
treatment: Apply strobilurin or triazole fungicides on a 14-day schedule; practice crop rotation with non-legume crops; plant resistant varieties where available; monitor leaf wetness and humidity

symptoms: Orange-brown pustules on lower leaf surfaces, yellowing and necrosis of infected leaf tissue, premature defoliation and reduced pod filling
treatment: Apply tebuconazole or chlorothalonil at first sign of disease; plant early-maturing varieties to escape late-season epidemics; bury crop residues to reduce overwintering inoculum; maintain field sanitation

symptoms: Olive-green to black velvety lesions on leaves and fruit, corky scabbed areas on mature fruit, premature leaf drop and reduced tree vigor
treatment: Apply captan or myclobutanil from green tip through harvest; plant scab-resistant varieties; rake and destroy fallen leaves in autumn; prune trees to improve air circulation

symptoms: Blossoms turning black and wilting rapidly, shoot tips bending into a shepherd's crook, oozing bacterial cankers on branches and trunk
treatment: Prune infected shoots at least 12 inches below visible symptoms during dormancy; apply streptomycin during bloom; plant resistant rootstocks and varieties; avoid excessive nitrogen and heavy pruning

symptoms: Bright yellow-orange spots on upper leaf surfaces, tiny black dots in center of lesions, raised cup-shaped structures on lower leaf surfaces
treatment: Apply myclobutanil or mancozeb beginning at pink bud stage; remove nearby cedar or juniper hosts where practical; plant resistant varieties like Liberty or Freedom; rake and remove fallen leaves
